channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert And Michelle R. Smith, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Early voting begins in Louisiana, Hawaii, Washington
item.description: Louisiana, Hawaii and Washington begin early voting on Friday, joining the vast majority of U.S. states in kicking off the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Return of 'candidate chaos'? Why some voters may see Vince Fong twice on their ballot
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- On Nov. 5, Congressman Vince Fong is hoping to lose -- well, in one of two races. That's because for some voters, his name will be on the ballot twice: once for Congress and once for State Assembly. Fong continues to emphasize he's only running for Congress.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris campaign features less talk of joy and more head-on digs at Trump as Election Day nears
item.description: LA CROSSE, Wis. (AP) — Joy Olson proudly wore a “Make America Joyful Again” button Thursday as she waited in line to attend a Kamala Harris rally.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump visits barbershop in the Bronx
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) – Former President Donald Trump visited a barbershop in the Bronx on Thursday. Trump stopped by Javiel's Barbershop on Castle Hill Avenue.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says the death of Hamas’ top leader, Yahya Sinwar, “gives us an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza.”.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump rally shooting review finds 'deep flaws' in Secret Service
item.description: An independent review of the July 13 assassination attempt of former President Trump offered a critical review of the Secret Service, said the episode “reveal[s] deep flaws” in the agency and called for new leadership for the nation’s protective service.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to avoid sharing election misinformation
item.description: The 2024 election season is here. As we’re waiting to learn the final results, it can be easy to be duped by false or misleading information. Here are four tips to help you avoid spreading election related misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Mormon voters set to play critical role in swing states
item.description: Mormon voters are increasingly seen as a crucial swing vote in the presidential election, with both Vice President Harris and former President Trump stepping up their outreach to the demographic in multiple battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Harris peppered with questions by Charlamagne tha God’s audience
item.description: Vice President Harris was peppered with questions Tuesday about her plan for Black Americans from radio host Charlamagne tha God and his listeners in an interview that’s part of a late-campaign media blitz before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Question 3: 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide
item.description: Dr. Sandra Cosgrove and Kerry Durmick joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to provide a proponent and opponent's view of one of the most contentious issues on the Nevada ballot in November: Question 3.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate Intel chair frets about foreign actors spreading hurricane, election misinformation
item.description: Senate Intelligence Committee Chair Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Tuesday indicated that he is concerned foreign actors are spreading misinformation related to the recovery from a pair of recent hurricanes and will be ramping up efforts amid the final election sprint.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Voting rights groups seek investigation into Wisconsin text message
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Voting rights advocates on Tuesday asked state and federal authorities to investigate anonymous text messages apparently targeting young Wisconsin voters, warning them not to vote in a state where they are ineligible.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump town hall ends with extended 'musical-fest' while he stands onstage
item.description: At a town hall in Oaks, Pa., on Monday, Trump ended the Q&A portion to ask the audience to remain and listen to music with him.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Walz to unveil Harris' plan for rural voters as campaign looks to cut into Trump's edge
item.description: Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz will unveil his ticket’s plans to improve the lives of rural voters as Vice President Kamala Harris looks to cut into former President Donald Trump’s support.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: In divided St. Cloud, challengers hope to flip House seats
item.description: While many legislative districts in Minnesota are either solidly Republican or DFL, in the St. Cloud, voters are evenly split. Races for the two House seats that represent the region are among the most competitive in the state.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lisa Cole, Nevada Assembly District 4 candidate, discusses economic development, affordable housing, and education
item.description: Lisa Cole, a candidate for Nevada Assembly District 4, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 voter-related lawsuits dropped in Nevada ahead of Election Day
item.description: Petitioners who filed three lawsuits challenging the maintenance of Nevada’s voter rolls have dismissed their cases, in part because voting by-mail ballot was already well underway.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC
